'''Shane Wright''' (born 13 March 1996) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Wright was born in [[Perth]], Western Australia. He played his junior rugby league for the [[Proserpine, Queensland#Sports and facilities|Proserpine Whitsunday Brahmans]] before being signed by the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] at the age of 14.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.whitsundaytimes.com.au/news/nrl-teams-sign-our-rising-stars/635823/|title=NRL teams sign our rising stars|first=Whitsunday|last=Times|newspaper=The Courier Mail|date=16 September 2010|publisher=|access-date=20 August 2017}}</ref> Wright then moved to the [[Gold Coast, Queensland]], attending [[Palm Beach Currumbin High School|Palm Beach Currumbin State High School]] and playing junior rugby league for the [[Burleigh Bears]] before being signed by the [[Gold Coast Titans]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/teenager-shane-wright-named-in-gold-coast-titans-squad-for-auckland-nines/news-story/48e343a3055851f4fbf6b0a11eb130da|title=Teenager Shane Wright named in Gold Coast Titans' squad for Auckland Nines|first=Emma|last=Greenwood|newspaper=Gold Coast Bulletin|date=29 January 2016|publisher=Gold CoastBulletin|access-date=20 August 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2015 and 2016, Wright played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/w/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161017074928/https://nycdatabase.org/players/w/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=17 October 2016|title=W|date=27 December 2012|publisher=|access-date=19 August 2017}}</ref> In November 2016, he signed a two-year contract with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] starting in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2016/11/07/_2017_cowboys_taking.html|title=2017 Cowboys taking shape|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|access-date=19 August 2017|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170813055654/http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2016/11/07/_2017_cowboys_taking.html|archive-date=13 August 2017|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

===2017===
In round 24 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Wright made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-cowboys-v-sharks/tabid/10874/newsid/111184/default.aspx|title=Updated team lists: Cowboys v Sharks|date=19 August 2017|publisher=|access-date=19 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2017/08/19/final_team_cowboys_v_1024858974.af_news.html|title=Final team: Cowboys v Sharks|first=NRL Digital|last=Media|publisher=|access-date=19 August 2017|archive-date=19 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170819145850/http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2017/08/19/final_team_cowboys_v_1024858974.af_news.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2018===
On April 17, Wright re-signed with the North Queensland club until the end of the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/04/17/building-young-stock--wright-clifford-re-sign/|title=Building young stock – Wright, Clifford re-sign|date=17 April 2018|publisher=|access-date=6 November 2018}}</ref> He spent the majority of the 2018 season playing for the [[Mackay Cutters]], registering just two NRL games for the North Queensland side, which included a start at second row in their Round 18 loss to the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/raiders-v-cowboys-team-lists-for-round-18-clash/news-story/78e42a1c3e84407e042b4a8741c1bef5|title=Team lists: Raiders v Cowboys|newspaper=Daily Telegraph|date=13 July 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
After starting the season playing for the [[Mackay Cutters]], Wright earned an NRL call-up in Round 11, coming off the bench in a 22–16 win over the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboys/cowboys-forward-shane-wright-up-for-middle-battle-against-titans/news-story/82ad1ec7976f1d3334570cd686c49af4|title = Subscribe to the Townsville Bulletin}}</ref> By Round 15, Wright had forced his way into the starting line up at second row, where he would remain for the rest of the season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboys/unheralded-cowboys-forward-shane-wright-on-the-rise/news-story/7942ab0f7bde45efd05bc4db3c17e0ad|title = Subscribe to the Townsville Bulletin}}</ref> In Round 21, he scored his first NRL try in a 14–18 loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboys/unheralded-cowboys-forward-shane-wright-on-the-rise/news-story/7942ab0f7bde45efd05bc4db3c17e0ad|title = Subscribe to the Townsville Bulletin}}</ref> In Round 24, Wright scored the final try at [[Willows Sports Complex]] in a 15–8 win over the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/08/29/wright-scores-the-last-try-at-1300smiles-stadium/|title = Wright scores the last try at 1300SMILES Stadium| date=29 August 2019 }}</ref> On 18 September, he was named the Cowboys' 2019 Rookie of the Year at the club's presentation night.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/09/18/wright-crowned-rookie-of-the-year/|title = Wright crowned Rookie of the Year|date = 18 September 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
In February, Wright was a member of the North Queensland club's [[2020 NRL Nines]] winning squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/02/15/nines-final-team-list-final-v-dragons/|title=Nines final team list: Final v Dragons|date=15 February 2020}}</ref> On 3 June, he re-signed with the North Queensland outfit until the end of the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/06/03/feldt-wright-re-sign-with-cowboys/|title=Feldt, Wright re-sign with Cowboys|date=3 June 2020|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref> Wright played just eight NRL games for North Queensland in 2020, starting six at {{rlp|sr}}.

===2021===
On 16 Oct 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=16 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=Shane Wright: Salford Red Devils sign North Queensland Cowboys back-rower on a two-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58917925}}</ref>

===2022 & 2023===
Wright played 12 games for Salford in the 2022 Super League season as they got to within one game of the grand final but were defeated 19-12 by St Helens. In the 2023 Super League season, Wright played 12 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

===2025===
On 29 August, it was reported that he had signed for [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] on loan for the remainder of the 2025 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 Aug 2025|accessdate=30 Aug 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=St Helens land Salford star as shock big name de-registered to facilitate transfer|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-land-salford-star-as-shock-big-name-de-registered-to-facilitate-transfer}}</ref>

In the elimination playoff game against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], Wright was called into the side as a replacement on the interchange bench. Wright went on to score the match winning try for St Helens after the full-time siren as they won 16-14.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce86r25lrxno/|title=Saints snatch stunning play-off victory at Leeds|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 29 September 2025 it was reported that he had signed a two-year permanent contract with St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 Sep 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=St Helens tie down overseas star following immortalising try as contract length confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-tie-down-overseas-star-following-immortalising-try-as-contract-length-confirmed}}</ref>
